introduction: This spec defines the OAuth authentication flow, user management endpoints, and API key lifecycle for af-hub. Note: requirements referencing google/uuid and crypto/rand APIs are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id: Idempotent endpoint that permanently revokes an API key by setting revoked_at.
glossary.Echo: Go HTTP framework used to implement the af-hub API server.
glossary.GET /api/v1/auth/providers: Public endpoint returning the list of configured OAuth providers with their authorize_url and scopes.
glossary.GET /api/v1/keys: Endpoint returning API key listings; admins see all keys, users see all their own historical keys.
glossary.GET /api/v1/users: Admin-only endpoint returning a flat list of all user records ordered by created_at ASC.
glossary.GET /api/v1/users/:id: Admin-only endpoint returning a single user record including provider_id and team_memberships.
glossary.POST /api/v1/auth/callback: Endpoint that accepts the OAuth authorization code, exchanges it with the provider, upserts the user, and issues a new API key.
glossary.POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh: Endpoint that generates a new secret for an existing key, reusing the original expiry duration.
glossary.POST /api/v1/users: Admin-only endpoint to pre-provision a user record without generating an API key.
glossary.PUT /api/v1/users/:id: Mixed-auth endpoint allowing full_name updates by any authenticated user on their own record, and status updates by admins.
glossary.active key: The single non-revoked, non-expired API key currently associated with a user; a new login revokes this key and creates a replacement.
glossary.af-hub: The platform being built; requires user identity before any capability can operate.
glossary.allowlist: Configured set of permitted redirect_uri origins against which incoming redirect_uri values are checked.
glossary.api_keys table: Database table storing API key records with fields: key_id, user_id, secret_hash, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at.
glossary.authorize_url: Base URL for the OAuth provider's authorization endpoint, returned by GET /api/v1/auth/providers.
glossary.base62: Encoding scheme using characters [0-9A-Za-z] producing alphanumeric-only output; used for key_id and secret generation.
glossary.crypto/rand: Go package providing cryptographically secure random bytes; required entropy source for key_id and secret generation.
glossary.dev mode: Server operating mode when external_url is absent from configuration; permits any http://localhost redirect_uri origin.
glossary.expires_at: Timestamp column indicating when an API key expires; null means the key is indefinite.
glossary.external_url: Server configuration value specifying the public-facing URL of the deployment; its presence or absence determines dev vs. production mode.
glossary.google/uuid: Go package used to generate UUID v4 user identifiers stored as lowercase hyphenated strings.
glossary.key_id: 8-character base62-encoded alphanumeric identifier for an API key, generated with crypto/rand entropy.
glossary.origin: The scheme + host + port portion of a URI used for allowlist matching (path component is excluded).
glossary.production mode: Server operating mode when external_url is present in configuration; requires exact origin match for redirect_uri validation.
glossary.provider: Named OAuth identity provider (e.g. 'github') whose implementation is registered in the provider registry.
glossary.provider registry: In-process mapping of provider names to their implementation (authorize URL construction, code exchange, userinfo extraction).
glossary.provider_id: Opaque string identifier assigned to a user by an OAuth provider (e.g. GitHub's integer user ID passed as a string); stored as TEXT.
glossary.redirect_uri: URI the OAuth provider redirects the user to after authorization; validated against the allowlist by origin matching.
glossary.revoked_at: Timestamp column set when an API key is permanently revoked; null means the key has not been revoked.
glossary.scopes: OAuth permission scopes requested from the provider; included in the provider registry entry and GET /api/v1/auth/providers response.
glossary.secret: 32-character base62-encoded alphanumeric string forming the credential portion of an API key token, generated with crypto/rand entropy and never stored in plaintext.
glossary.secret_hash: SHA-256 hash of the API key secret stored in the api_keys table; the plaintext secret is never persisted.
glossary.server_foundation: Upstream spec providing auth middleware, database schema (users and api_keys tables), and structured error response helpers.
glossary.state: CSRF protection parameter generated and validated by the CLI client; the server passes it through without storing or validating it.
glossary.status: User account state; either 'active' or 'blocked'. Blocked users cannot authenticate via OAuth callback.
glossary.team_memberships: Array of team membership objects (team_id, team_name, role) returned in GET /api/v1/users/:id response; values owned by spec 3.
glossary.token: The full API key string in the format `af_<key_id>_<secret>` returned to the user at creation or refresh time.
glossary.token_url: URL used server-side to exchange an authorization code for an access token with the OAuth provider.
glossary.updated_at: Timestamp column updated only when at least one field value actually changes; no-op writes do not bump this value.
glossary.upsert: Insert-or-update operation keyed on (provider, provider_id) that creates a new user record if none exists, or updates fields of an existing record.
glossary.userinfo_url: URL used server-side to fetch the authenticated user's profile from the OAuth provider after code exchange.
glossary.users table: Database table storing user records with fields: id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, provider_id, created_at, updated_at.
glossary.email: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.expires: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.full_name: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.id: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.math/rand: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.name: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.providers: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.username: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.users: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.

user_story.goal: register OAuth providers in a pluggable registry with configurable URLs and scopes
user_story.benefit: new providers can be added without changing the core authentication flow

acceptance_criteria.action: stores each provider entry with name, `authorize_url`, `token_url`, `userinfo_url`, and `scopes`; GitHub ships as the first provider with default URLs and default `scopes` of "user:email"
uses the configured override values for `authorize_url`, `token_url`, or `userinfo_url` in place of provider defaults
acceptance_criteria.feature: optional URL overrides are configured for a provider

acceptance_criteria.action: allows a new provider to be registered by adding it to the registry without modifying the authentication flow code
returns HTTP 200 with a JSON object containing a `providers` array; each entry includes `name`, base `authorize_url` (no `state` parameter embedded), and `scopes`; no client secrets are included
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {"providers": [{"name": string, "authorize_url": string, "scopes": string}]}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `GET /api/v1/auth/providers` is called

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 200 with an empty `providers` array
edge_cases.trigger: `GET /api/v1/auth/providers` is called when no providers are registered

requirements.title: OAuth Callback — Code Exchange and User Upsert

user_story.goal: complete the OAuth authorization code flow via the callback endpoint
user_story.benefit: I receive a user record and a new API key without managing passwords

acceptance_criteria.action: validates `provider` against the `provider registry`, validates `expires` (defaulting to 90 if omitted), validates `redirect_uri` `origin` against the `allowlist`, exchanges the code with the provider, upserts the user, revokes any existing `active key`, creates a new API key, and returns the user object and new key — all user upsert and key operations execute within a single database transaction
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body containing user object and api_key object including plaintext `secret` and `token`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` is called with a valid JSON body containing recognized `provider`, valid `expires`, and an allowlisted `redirect_uri`

acceptance_criteria.action: creates a new user record with `status` 'active', a new UUID v4 `id` generated via `google/uuid`, and stores `provider_id` from the provider response
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with newly created user in response body
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` is called and no user exists for the (provider, provider_id) pair

acceptance_criteria.action: updates `username` and `email` from the current provider response without modifying `provider_id`; `updated_at` is bumped only if at least one field value actually changes
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with updated user in response body
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` is called and a user already exists for the (provider, provider_id) pair

acceptance_criteria.action: generates `key_id` (8 alphanumeric chars, base62, `crypto/rand`) and `secret` (32 alphanumeric chars, base62, `crypto/rand`), stores SHA-256 hash of the secret in the `api_keys table`, computes `expires_at` as now + 24h × N days (null when expires=0), and returns the plaintext `token` in format `af_<key_id>_<secret>`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: api_key object with key_id, secret (plaintext, 32 chars), token, user_id, created_at, expires_at
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a new API key is created during `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `revoked_at` on the previous key before creating the new key, within the same database transaction; if no active key exists this revocation step is a no-op and a new key is still created
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a new API key is created during `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` and an `active key` already exists for the user

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with a structured error body (per `server_foundation` error format) describing the unrecognized provider value
edge_cases.error_condition: `provider` field in `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` body does not match any registered provider

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with a structured error body describing the invalid field and accepted values; does not proceed to code exchange
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 400 with structured error body listing accepted values [0, 30, 60, 90]
edge_cases.error_condition: `expires` field in `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` body is supplied but is not one of 0, 30, 60, or 90

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with a structured error body; does not proceed to code exchange
edge_cases.error_condition: `redirect_uri` `origin` in `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` body is not on the `allowlist`

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 502 with a structured error body signaling upstream provider failure; the database transaction is not started
edge_cases.error_condition: the OAuth provider code exchange fails (provider unreachable, returns an error, or response cannot be parsed)

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with a structured error body; no user upsert or key creation is performed
edge_cases.error_condition: the OAuth provider returns a null or empty email for the user

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with a structured error body describing the validation failure; no user upsert or key creation is performed
edge_cases.error_condition: the `username` derived from the provider (GitHub login field) contains characters other than alphanumerics and hyphens, or exceeds 39 characters

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 409 with a structured error body; no user upsert or key creation is performed; admin must resolve the conflict manually
edge_cases.error_condition: the derived `username` (compared case-insensitively) matches an existing user record belonging to a different (provider, provider_id) pair

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body; does not generate a new API key or reactivate the user
edge_cases.error_condition: the user found for the (provider, provider_id) pair has `status` 'blocked'

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with a structured error body indicating a configuration error; does not fall back to a permissive `allowlist`
edge_cases.error_condition: `external_url` is absent from configuration and the server is not in `dev mode` (i.e. a production deployment without `external_url` set)

edge_cases.action: rolls back the entire transaction leaving the database in its pre-callback state; returns HTTP 500 with a structured error body
edge_cases.error_condition: the database transaction covering user upsert, key revocation, and key creation fails at any step

edge_cases.action: cancels the outbound HTTP request using a context deadline, returns HTTP 502 with a structured error body; no database transaction is started
edge_cases.error_condition: the OAuth provider code exchange call does not return within an acceptable timeout window

user_story.goal: ensure only permitted redirect URIs are accepted during OAuth callback
user_story.benefit: open redirect attacks are prevented even in development environments

acceptance_criteria.action: allows any `redirect_uri` whose `origin` is `http://localhost` (any port); rejects URIs with hosts such as `http://localhost.evil.com` or any non-localhost host
allows only `redirect_uri` values whose scheme + host + port exactly match the configured `external_url` value; path component is not considered in the comparison
extracts the `origin` (scheme + host + port) from the incoming `redirect_uri` and checks it against the `allowlist`; the path component is never included in this comparison

edge_cases.action: rejects the URI; does not treat it as a localhost match in `dev mode`
edge_cases.error_condition: `redirect_uri` host is `http://localhost.evil.com` or any hostname that merely contains 'localhost' as a substring

user_story.goal: pre-provision user records before those users have logged in
user_story.benefit: users can be set up with correct team memberships before their first OAuth login

acceptance_criteria.action: creates a new user record with `status` 'active', a UUID v4 `id` generated via `google/uuid`, no associated API key, and returns HTTP 201 with the user object excluding `provider_id`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 with user object: {id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, created_at, updated_at} — provider_id omitted
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `POST /api/v1/users` is called by an admin with a valid request body

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts any non-empty string as the `provider` field on `POST /api/v1/users`; logs a warning if the value does not match a registered provider but still creates the user
enforces that `username` on `POST /api/v1/users` contains only alphanumeric characters and hyphens, and is at most 39 characters; enforces `provider_id` is non-empty
enforces case-insensitive uniqueness of `username` and uniqueness of (provider, provider_id) pair across all user records

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with a structured error body describing the validation failure; no user record is created
edge_cases.error_condition: `username` on `POST /api/v1/users` fails validation (invalid characters or exceeds 39 characters) or `provider_id` is empty

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 409 with a structured error body; no user record is created
edge_cases.error_condition: `username` on `POST /api/v1/users` matches an existing username case-insensitively, or the (provider, provider_id) pair already exists

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body; no user record is created
edge_cases.error_condition: `POST /api/v1/users` is called by a non-admin authenticated user

user_story.goal: retrieve a flat list of all user records
user_story.benefit: I can audit and manage all users on the platform

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with a JSON object containing a `users` array of all user records ordered by created_at ASC; each entry includes id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, created_at, updated_at; `provider_id` is omitted
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {"users": [{id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, created_at, updated_at}]}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `GET /api/v1/users` is called by an admin

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with an empty `users` array
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {"users": []}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `GET /api/v1/users` is called by an admin and no user records exist

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body
edge_cases.error_condition: `GET /api/v1/users` is called by a non-admin authenticated user

user_story.goal: retrieve a single user's full record including team memberships
user_story.benefit: I can inspect a specific user's details and group associations

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with the full user object including `provider_id` and a `team_memberships` array; each membership entry contains team_id, team_name, and role (treated as opaque string values owned by spec 3)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, provider_id, created_at, updated_at, team_memberships: [{team_id, team_name, role}]}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `GET /api/v1/users/:id` is called by an admin for an existing user

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 404 with a structured error body per `server_foundation` format
edge_cases.error_condition: `GET /api/v1/users/:id` is called for a user ID that does not exist

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body
edge_cases.error_condition: `GET /api/v1/users/:id` is called by a non-admin authenticated user

user_story.goal: update my own full_name, or as an admin update any user's status or full_name
user_story.benefit: user records stay current without requiring re-authentication or admin intervention for display name changes

acceptance_criteria.action: updates `full_name` on the user record; sets it to null if the supplied value is null or empty string; bumps `updated_at` only if the new value differs from the stored value; returns HTTP 200 with the updated user object including `provider_id` but excluding `team_memberships`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, provider_id, created_at, updated_at}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` is called by any authenticated user to update their own `full_name`

acceptance_criteria.action: updates `status` to the supplied value ('active' or 'blocked'); bumps `updated_at` only if the new value differs from the stored value; returns HTTP 200 with the updated user object including `provider_id`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, provider_id, created_at, updated_at}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` is called by an admin to update `status` on any user record

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with the current unmodified user object; does not bump `updated_at`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with current unmodified user object including provider_id
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` is called with a request body containing no recognized fields, or all recognized fields identical to current stored values

acceptance_criteria.action: treats an admin caller's own `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` request with admin-level permissions; the admin may set `status` on their own record

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body; no update is performed
edge_cases.error_condition: a non-admin user calls `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` on another user's record

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body; no update is performed
edge_cases.error_condition: a non-admin user calls `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` and the request body includes a `status` field (regardless of other fields present)

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 404 with a structured error body per `server_foundation` format
edge_cases.error_condition: `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` is called for a user ID that does not exist

user_story.goal: retrieve a listing of API keys
user_story.benefit: I can audit active and historical keys without exposing plaintext secrets

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with all API keys across all users ordered by created_at ASC; each entry includes key_id, user_id, created_at, `expires_at`, `revoked_at`; `secret` is never included
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {"keys": [{key_id, user_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at}]}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `GET /api/v1/keys` is called with an admin token

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with all keys ever associated with the requesting user (including expired and revoked historical keys) ordered by created_at ASC; `secret` is never included
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {"keys": [{key_id, user_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at}]}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `GET /api/v1/keys` is called with a user API key

acceptance_criteria.action: never includes a computed 'status' or 'is_active' field in key listing responses; consumers determine key validity by inspecting `expires_at` and `revoked_at` directly

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body
edge_cases.error_condition: `GET /api/v1/keys` is called with a workspace token

user_story.goal: rotate the secret for an existing API key without changing its key_id or expiry duration
user_story.benefit: I can recover from a compromised secret without re-authenticating via OAuth

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a new `secret` (32 alphanumeric chars, base62, `crypto/rand`), stores its SHA-256 hash as `secret_hash` in the `api_keys table`, recalculates `expires_at` using the original expiry duration (null stays null; timed keys: now + original N days), and returns HTTP 200 with the key object including new plaintext `secret` and `token`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with body: {key_id, user_id, secret (plaintext), token (af_<key_id>_<secret>), created_at, expires_at, revoked_at: null}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` is called by an authorized caller for an existing, non-revoked key

acceptance_criteria.action: permits the refresh; generates a new `secret`, recalculates `expires_at` as now + original N days, and returns HTTP 200 — the caller is not required to re-authenticate via OAuth
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with updated key object including new plaintext secret
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` is called for an expired (but not revoked) key

acceptance_criteria.action: permits the refresh; user `status` does not gate administrative key management operations
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with updated key object including new plaintext secret
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` is called by an admin for a key belonging to a blocked user

acceptance_criteria.action: never allows the caller to override the expiry duration at refresh time; the original expiry duration is always reused

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 404 with a structured error body per `server_foundation` format; revoked keys are treated as non-existent for refresh operations
edge_cases.error_condition: `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` is called for a `key_id` that does not exist or has been revoked

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body; no secret is rotated
edge_cases.error_condition: a non-admin user calls `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` for a key belonging to a different user

user_story.benefit: a compromised or unused key can be invalidated immediately and cannot be used again

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `revoked_at` to the current timestamp on first revocation and returns HTTP 204 with no body
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 204 with no body
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` is called by an authorized caller for an existing key

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 204 with no body (idempotent no-op); does not update `revoked_at` again
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 204 with no body
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` is called for a key that has already been revoked

acceptance_criteria.action: permits the revocation; user `status` does not gate administrative key management operations
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 204 with no body
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` is called by an admin for a key belonging to a blocked user

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 404 with a structured error body per `server_foundation` format
edge_cases.error_condition: `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` is called for a `key_id` that does not exist in the `api_keys table`

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 403 with a structured error body; `revoked_at` is not set
edge_cases.error_condition: a non-admin user calls `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` for a key belonging to a different user

user_story.goal: ensure API key material is generated with cryptographically secure entropy and stored safely
user_story.benefit: keys cannot be predicted or brute-forced from stored hashes

acceptance_criteria.action: generates all `key_id` and `secret` values using `crypto/rand` as the sole entropy source; `math/rand` or any deterministic source is not used

stores only the SHA-256 hash of the `secret` in the `api_keys table` as `secret_hash`; the plaintext `secret` is returned only at key creation or refresh time and is never written to persistent storage
encodes `key_id` as 8 characters and `secret` as 32 characters using `base62` encoding (characters [0-9A-Za-z] only) producing the `token` in format `af_<key_id>_<secret>`

user_story.goal: enforce consistent username format across all creation paths
user_story.benefit: usernames from OAuth providers and admin creation remain compatible with GitHub conventions and are safely stored

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts a `username` only if it contains exclusively alphanumeric characters and hyphens, and is at most 39 characters in length; this rule applies to both `POST /api/v1/users` and the derived GitHub login field in `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`
stores `username` exactly as supplied (preserving original casing) but enforces uniqueness by comparing lowercased forms

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with a structured error body; the user record is not created or updated
edge_cases.error_condition: `username` contains spaces, underscores, or any character that is not alphanumeric or a hyphen

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with a structured error body; the user record is not created or updated
edge_cases.error_condition: `username` exceeds 39 characters

user_story.goal: ensure updated_at is only bumped when field values actually change
user_story.benefit: the audit trail remains meaningful and cache invalidation is not triggered by no-op writes

acceptance_criteria.action: updates `updated_at` on a user record only when at least one field value in the write differs from the currently stored value; no-op writes (fields absent or identical to stored values) do not modify `updated_at`

correctness_properties.title: One Active Key Per User
correctness_properties.invariant: at most one API key row in the `api_keys table` for that user has both `revoked_at` IS NULL and (`expires_at` IS NULL OR `expires_at` > now())

correctness_properties.title: Secret Never Stored in Plaintext
correctness_properties.for_any: API key row in the `api_keys table`
correctness_properties.invariant: the `secret_hash` column contains the SHA-256 hash of the `secret`; the plaintext `secret` value does not appear in any database column

correctness_properties.title: Revocation is Permanent
correctness_properties.for_any: API key with a non-null `revoked_at` value
correctness_properties.invariant: no subsequent operation (refresh or re-authentication) can clear `revoked_at` or restore the key to active status

correctness_properties.title: Username Uniqueness is Case-Insensitive
correctness_properties.for_any: pair of distinct user rows in the `users table`
correctness_properties.invariant: no two rows have `username` values that are equal when both are lowercased

correctness_properties.title: Login Transaction Atomicity
correctness_properties.for_any: invocation of `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` that reaches the database write phase
correctness_properties.invariant: either all three operations (user upsert, previous key revocation, new key creation) are committed together, or none of them are; no partial state is observable in the `users table` or `api_keys table`

correctness_properties.title: Crypto Entropy Source Exclusivity
correctness_properties.invariant: the value is produced using `crypto/rand` as the entropy source and `base62` encoding; no call to `math/rand` or any seeded deterministic generator is made in the key generation code path

correctness_properties.title: updated_at Not Bumped on No-op Writes
correctness_properties.for_any: write to the `users table` where all supplied field values equal the currently stored values
correctness_properties.invariant: `updated_at` retains its pre-write value after the operation completes

correctness_properties.title: provider_id Immutable After Creation
correctness_properties.for_any: user row in the `users table` that was created via `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`
correctness_properties.invariant: the `provider_id` column value is identical to the value stored at initial user creation and is never updated by subsequent callback invocations for the same (provider, provider_id) pair

steps.action: calls `GET /api/v1/auth/providers` to retrieve the GitHub `authorize_url` and `scopes`
returns the provider list including GitHub `authorize_url` and `scopes` value ("user:email")
generates a random `state` value, appends `state`, `client_id`, `redirect_uri`, and `scope` to `authorize_url`, and redirects the user's browser to GitHub
authenticates the user and redirects to the CLI's `redirect_uri` with an authorization code and the original `state`
validates the returned `state` matches the generated value, then calls `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` with provider='github', code, redirect_uri, and optional expires
validates `provider`, `expires`, and `redirect_uri` origin against the `allowlist`
exchanges the authorization code with GitHub via `token_url`, then fetches user info from `userinfo_url`
validates the derived `username` and email from the provider response
opens a database transaction: upserts the user record in the `users table`, revokes any existing `active key` in the `api_keys table`, and creates a new API key with `secret_hash` stored
commits the transaction and returns HTTP 200 with the user object and new `token` (plaintext `secret` included, only time it is returned)
stores the returned `token` locally for subsequent authenticated API calls

execution_paths.title: Admin Pre-provisions a User Who Later Logs In

steps.action: calls `POST /api/v1/users` with username, email, provider, and provider_id to create a user record with no API key
validates username format, checks uniqueness of username (case-insensitive) and (provider, provider_id), creates the user with status 'active', returns HTTP 201 with the user object
later completes the GitHub OAuth flow via the CLI, which calls `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` with the matching provider and provider_id
finds the existing user record via (provider, provider_id) upsert, creates a new API key (expires defaults to 90 days), returns HTTP 200 with user object and new token

execution_paths.title: User Refreshes an Expired API Key

steps.action: calls `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` for their own expired (non-revoked) key
verifies the key exists in the `api_keys table` and is not revoked; confirms the caller owns the key
generates a new `secret` using `crypto/rand` and `base62` encoding, stores the SHA-256 hash as `secret_hash`, recalculates `expires_at` using the original expiry duration
returns HTTP 200 with the updated key object including the new plaintext `secret` and `token`
stores the new `token` and uses it for subsequent authenticated API calls without re-authenticating via OAuth

execution_paths.title: Admin Blocks a User and Revokes Their Key

steps.action: calls `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` with status='blocked' to block the target user
validates admin token, updates `status` to 'blocked' on the user record, bumps `updated_at`, returns HTTP 200 with updated user object
calls `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` to revoke the blocked user's API key
sets `revoked_at` on the key in the `api_keys table` and returns HTTP 204; subsequent authentication attempts with this key are rejected by the `server_foundation` middleware

error_handling.condition: Unrecognized `provider` value in `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 400 with structured error body describing the unrecognized provider; do not proceed to code exchange

error_handling.condition: `expires` value in `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` is supplied but is not 0, 30, 60, or 90
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 400 with structured error body listing accepted values; do not proceed to code exchange

error_handling.condition: `redirect_uri` `origin` not on the `allowlist`
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 400 with structured error body; do not proceed to code exchange

error_handling.condition: OAuth provider code exchange fails (unreachable, error response, or unparseable response)
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 502 with structured error body signaling upstream provider failure; no database writes are performed

error_handling.condition: OAuth provider returns null or empty email
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 400 with structured error body; no user upsert or key creation is performed

error_handling.condition: Derived `username` from provider fails validation (invalid characters or length > 39)
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 400 with structured error body describing validation failure; no user upsert or key creation is performed

error_handling.condition: Derived `username` conflicts (case-insensitive) with a different (provider, provider_id) pair
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 409 with structured error body; no user upsert or key creation is performed

error_handling.condition: User found for (provider, provider_id) has `status` 'blocked'
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body; no key is generated and user is not reactivated

error_handling.condition: `external_url` absent in a production deployment (configuration error)
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 500 with structured error body; do not fall back to permissive `allowlist`

error_handling.condition: Database transaction covering user upsert, key revocation, and key creation fails at any step
error_handling.behavior: Roll back entire transaction; return HTTP 500 with structured error body; database state is unchanged

error_handling.condition: OAuth provider code exchange call exceeds timeout deadline
error_handling.behavior: Cancel the outbound HTTP request via context deadline; return HTTP 502 with structured error body; no database transaction is started

error_handling.condition: `username` on `POST /api/v1/users` fails validation or `provider_id` is empty
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 400 with structured error body; no user record is created

error_handling.condition: `username` or (provider, provider_id) pair already exists on `POST /api/v1/users`
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 409 with structured error body; no user record is created

error_handling.condition: `POST /api/v1/users` called by a non-admin
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body; no user record is created

error_handling.condition: `GET /api/v1/users` called by a non-admin
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body

error_handling.condition: `GET /api/v1/users/:id` called for a non-existent user ID
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 404 with structured error body per `server_foundation` format

error_handling.condition: `GET /api/v1/users/:id` called by a non-admin
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body

error_handling.condition: Non-admin calls `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` on another user's record
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body; no update is performed

error_handling.condition: Non-admin includes `status` field in `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` request body
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body; no update is performed

error_handling.condition: `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` called for a non-existent user ID
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 404 with structured error body per `server_foundation` format

error_handling.condition: `GET /api/v1/keys` called with a workspace token
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body

error_handling.condition: `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` called for a `key_id` that does not exist or has been revoked
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 404 with structured error body per `server_foundation` format

error_handling.condition: Non-admin calls `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` for another user's key
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body; no secret is rotated

error_handling.condition: `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` called for a `key_id` that does not exist in the `api_keys table`
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 404 with structured error body per `server_foundation` format

error_handling.condition: Non-admin calls `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` for another user's key
error_handling.behavior: Return HTTP 403 with structured error body; `revoked_at` is not set

error_handling.condition: `redirect_uri` host contains 'localhost' as a substring but is not exactly `http://localhost` (e.g. `http://localhost.evil.com`)
error_handling.behavior: Reject the URI with HTTP 400; do not treat it as a localhost match in `dev mode`

external_apis.version: unverified — cross-check installed version before implementation

symbols.name: New
symbols.notes: Generates a UUID v4. Returns a UUID type that can be rendered as a lowercase hyphenated string via .String().

symbols.name: Read
symbols.notes: Fills b with cryptographically secure random bytes. Required entropy source for key_id and secret generation. math/rand must not be used.
